title: × graptoveria calva
intro: × Graptoveria calva, also known as a hybrid succulent plant, is a cross between Graptopetalum paraguayense and Echeveria calva. Traditionally, the sap and roots of × Graptoveria calva have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of flavonoids and glycosides. The medicinal properties of × Graptoveria calva are believed to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the sap and roots of × Graptoveria calva are often used to create salves, tinctures, and infusions for topical application and internal consumption.
